Problem: Many users (47%) do not return to Method after the session they synced.
Solution: Monthly Insights email that will support the product engagement loops and lead users to engage with the Home Dashboard.
Opportunity:
-Motivate and give a reason for users to come back to Method to accomplish something.
-Leverage their trial by reaching them outside the platform after they synced.
-Improve external communications to bring users back to the product.
Core Metric & KPIs:
ARR
1.Improve 2nd Visit
2.Churn related too:
- Not using their Method Account
- Failure to adopt, train, and engage CRM users.
3. New Metric Email Engagement (Open Rate, CTR, Unsubs)
Target Improvement (Estimate):
2nd Visit Rate: +8.7% (57% to 62%)
Churn: save 6 /month or 78/year (2.3 to 2.24%)

Problem: Apps are missing intro state, making it harder for a new user to understand the value quickly.
Solution: Empty state and first visit intro/value proposition into applications. Data or Not, value can still be shown better.
Opportunity: Ensure the first visit to any app conveys the app's value and purpose clearly to boost discovery and activation. Can be layered on top of an empty state.
Core Metric & KPIs:
ARR
1. 2nd visit rate 
- Increase chances of finding value on first visit
2. Trial activation rate
3. Time to first value - Key conversion steps
4. Conversion to subscribe
Mechanism - Why we believe this will mov: Reduce time to value - Empty first impressions are abandonment points—transforming blank screens and incomplete data into clear value propositions.
Target Improvement (Estimate):
2nd Visit Rate: +8.2% (57 to 61.7)
Trial activation: +13.2% (44% to 50%)

Problem: On initial onboarding, lots of accounts do not declare Industry and role.
Solution: Require Industry and job data. Or ask for it at a different time OR access industry data from elsewhere. True solution TBD.
Opportunity: Opportunity to get clear visibility and reliable data on industry and user role and open the posibility of greater product and content targetting.
Core Metric & KPIs:
1. Data quality and reliability
2. Data availability/timing
Mechanism - Why we believe this will mov: Relevance - Enables stronger targeting initiatives for activation.

Problem: Appcues onboarding checklistis generic, not dynamic and doesn't recognize completion of the steps unless initated from the onboarding checklist (It limiting)
Solution: Build the integrated checklist that can support more dynamic and targeted content. Allow completion of key tasks even when not initiated from the checklist. Use as a compass that live on the home screen for the home
Opportunity: Make onboarding feel built in and responsive to new users actions to support all future targeting initialived and improve first impression UX.
Core Metric & KPIs:
ARR
1. 2nd visit rate 
- Integrated experience for improved first impression
2. Checklist completion rate
- supports activation and driving subscriptions
3. Key action completion rate
- finding value with key activation moments
(Also: Feature stability)
Mechanism - Why we believe this will mov: Guidance & Integration - Provides a clear path and feedback for the platform's value driving actions.
Target Improvement (Estimate):
2nd Visit Rate: +13.2% (57% to 65%)
Checklist Completion: 12x increase from today - 15% (vs. 1.2% Appcues)

Solution: Industry specifc Onboarding. Target the MWD industries first.
Opportunity: The opportunity is to provide industry and role-specific onboarding tasks so that users immediately see Method's value for their specific business context. Make key value adds more obvious.
Core Metric & KPIs:
ARR

- Increase chances of finding value through key value drivers
2. Trial activation rate
- ICP targeted for reaching key value actions for their vertical
3. Conversion to subscribe
Mechanism - Why we believe this will mov: Relevance - Highly relevant feature discovery through a guided checklist experience.

Solution: More insights to appeal to our ICP/ leverage smart defaults. MWD focus.
Opportunity: Share insights that targets insistry, jobs or roles to activate users upon landing in the Method.
Core Metric & KPIs:
ARR
1. Insights engagement - weekly usage.
-Activate/adoption 
2. Churn improvements to:
-  Failure to adopt, train, and engage CRM users. 
3. 2nd visit rate

Problem: Users are not supported by the product when in comes to deeper addoption. No guidance - unless they meet with PS or CSM.
Solution:
Recommendations V1 - Recommendations for highly relivant discovery, setup and adoption tasks
 - EDA tiggers if possible
Opportunity: Provide product lead help, guidance, recommendations that is relevant to the industry, actions already taken and other data we have gathered to deepen their product discovery and adoption.
Core Metric & KPIs:
ARR/GRR
1.Trial activation rate 
- assitsted activation and adoption
2.Churn improvements to:
- Not using their Method Account
- Failure to adopt, train, and engage CRM users.
3. Reduce CSM calls
4. Recommendation completion rate
Mechanism - Why we believe this will mov: Smart guidance - surfacing relevant next steps, setup and optimization to target activation and deeper adoption.

Trial activation: +11.5% growth in trial activation (44% → 49.1%) - activate 22 new users a month. 

Churn: reduce by 1-2% (Need more data)

Project: In product message - Add your CC "Don't lose access"
Solution: Mid-trial in product message - Ask for payment before trial ends. “Don’t lose progress“ - subscriber now
Opportunity: Remind users to subscribe and of what they will lose without subscribing
Mechanism - Why we believe this will mov: Encourge signing up before the trial is over.

Project: Upset to new app packs and workflows
Problem: Apps and packs that are not downloaded are not well exposed or optimized for upselling and gaining new users.
Solution: -- TBD--
Opportunity: Opportunity to Explore the app packs more and improve their value proposition to users without the app

Solution: Simplified pricing structure + check click checkout (Stripe experience)
Opportunity: Given users an easy to understand pricing structure that helps the user envision afordability and do simple future cost analysis.
Mechanism - Why we believe this will mov: Make subscribe process easier to understand and simple/fast to prepare your costs before paying.

Project: Default to monthly costs of an annual subscription
Solution: Default to the monthly cost view  Or adjust the the Annual cost to a “Per Month” breakdown to reduce sticker shock.
Opportunity: Avoid sticker shock - still promote annual pre-pay value
Mechanism - Why we believe this will mov: Make subscribe process easier to understand - less intimidating.

Project: Add CC incentive messsage in product.
Problem: Payment needed before user in confident.
Solution: Offer $ incentive to add card early
Opportunity: Get early commitment even withou confidence / Switch to Opt-out structure early-mid trial.
Mechanism - Why we believe this will mov: Incentives for early subscription and ensure we are asking for CC before trial ends.

Solution: Add more Context and clarity to how pricing works.
Opportunity: provide help and clarity on screen and reduce reliance on support.
Mechanism - Why we believe this will mov: Make subscribe process easier to understand with better explanations

Project: Make the subscription editing flow faster - only offer change that impacts price
Solution: Flow for quick add/remove apps and user - to make the final pricing easier to get to. Fast clarity.
Opportunity: Make the subscription cost preparation faster with lower friction to improve subscribe success rate without help.
Mechanism - Why we believe this will mov: Make subscribe process easier to drive more subscriptions.

Project: Trade CC for a Trial extention (Opt out model)
Solution:
Offer trial extension if credit card is added. (Transition to opt-out structure)
“Get another 14 day free - with CC added”
Opportunity: increase subscription commitment during trail but offering more trial time (Which we do provide sometimes)
Mechanism - Why we believe this will mov: Incentivise putting a credit card down - leading them to be in an Opt-out product state that typically dives higher subscriptions.

Project: Trade tasks for more trial time (Earned extention)
Problem: Product is removed at moment the decision is needed
Solution: "Earned Extension" The Idea: "Need 7 more days? Invite a team member, send your first invoice and we'll extend your trial automatically."
Opportunity: Incentivize key value actions at trial end and given users more time to make their decision.
Mechanism - Why we believe this will mov: Incentivize deeper product usage that may help them see value and subscribe.

Solution: Add the “Add your payment” as a natural next step after completing key value moments in the onboarding.
Opportunity: Leverage users that want guidance to encourge adding payment as a natural next step.
Mechanism - Why we believe this will mov: Encourge signing up before the trial is over

Problem: Pricing unclear / complex  leading to hesitation
Solution: Provide a seperate "Add payment" flow
Opportunity: Make adding the credits card less complex and specific to the subscription costs (Apps/users)
Mechanism - Why we believe this will mov: Make subscribe process easier to drive more subscriptions.

Project: Lock screen is focused on all the time and effort the user saved.
Solution: End trial with new focus of subscribe page “Trial summary - focus on all the value they received” and are giving up
Opportunity: Opportunity to improve perception of trial end and share key value propositions without access to the full platform.
Mechanism - Why we believe this will mov: Help users find value even after being locked out of the functional platform.

Solution: Highlight value adds, case studies, FOMO tactics and new product driven work flows that make them want to log back in.
